name: cf-verification

criteria.name: all_checks_run
criteria.description: All relevant verification checks are executed (tests, build, lint, format)

scoring.0: No verification checks run — just claimed 'done'
scoring.1: Only one check run (e.g., only tests but no build or lint)
scoring.2: Most checks run but one relevant check was skipped
scoring.3: All applicable checks run: tests, build, lint, and format verification

automated_check.command: npm test && npm run build && npm run lint

criteria.name: no_false_passes
criteria.description: Does not report passing when there are actual failures

scoring.0: Reports all-clear when there are obvious test failures or build errors
scoring.1: Reports passing but ignores warnings that indicate real problems
scoring.2: Correctly reports failures but mischaracterizes their severity
scoring.3: Accurately reports all results — failures, warnings, and passes — with correct severity

criteria.name: clear_report
criteria.description: Verification results are presented in a clear, scannable format

scoring.0: Raw command output dumped with no summary
scoring.1: Some summary but hard to quickly determine pass/fail status
scoring.2: Clear pass/fail for each check but missing details on failures
scoring.3: Clear pass/fail status for each check with concise failure details and suggested fixes

criteria.name: actionable_failures
criteria.description: When checks fail, the report provides actionable next steps

scoring.0: Failures reported with no guidance on how to fix them
scoring.1: Generic advice given (e.g., 'fix the tests') without specifics
scoring.2: Specific failures identified but fix suggestions could be more actionable
scoring.3: Each failure includes the specific error, affected file/line, and a concrete fix suggestion

notes: Verification is the final gate before claiming a task is done. The most critical criterion is no_false_passes — a verification that claims green when it is red is worse than no verification at all.
